title: Pan-Seared Gyoza with Crispy Steak
description: A fusion of Japanese flavors with a crispy twist, this dish combines savory gyoza with a tender steak crust.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the steak with salt, pepper, and sesame seeds.
Sear the steak for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the steak to a plate and let it rest for 5 minutes.
In the same skillet, add the crispy garlic oil and cook for 1 minute.
Add the gyoza wrappers and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the gyoza from the skillet and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In the same skillet, add the soy sauce, sesame oil, grated ginger, and chopped scallions.
Stir to combine and bring to a simmer.
Return the gyoza to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until they are coated in the sauce.
Slice the steak into thin strips and serve with the crispy gyoza and sauce.
Garnish with additional scallions and sesame seeds if desired.
